caption_gen: The epidermis shows mild atypia. The dermis contains a dense infiltrate of atypical melanocytes with irregular nuclear contours and prominent nucleoli, arranged in nests and sheets. Diagnosis: Melanoma.

The image shows a dermal proliferation of melanocytes with atypical features including irregular nuclear contours and prominent nucleoli. There is also evidence of pigment variation.

Melanoma.
Basal cell hyperplasia and increased melanin pigment are present. The diagnosis is hyperpigmentation.

Description: The image shows a dermal lesion with atypical melanocytes infiltrating the dermis, forming irregular nests and linear arrangements. There is also evidence of dermal fibrosis.
Diagnosis: Melanoma.
The epidermis shows a compound nevus pattern with nests of melanocytes. There is mild atypia in the melanocytes. The dermis shows a prominent lymphocytic infiltrate. Diagnosis: Compound nevus.

The image shows a dense proliferation of spindle cells with elongated nuclei and eosinophilic cytoplasm. There is a prominent inflammatory infiltrate. The spindle cells are arranged in fascicles. The diagnosis is MELANOMA.

The epidermis shows a regular pattern of nesting melanocytes. There is a prominent pigment network with irregular holes and thickened lines. There is a lack of maturation and a high mitotic rate.

Melanoma.

The image shows a dermal proliferation of melanocytic nests. Some nests exhibit atypical nuclear features.
Melanoma.

The image shows a dermal nevus with a compound pattern. There are dermal papillae with melanocytes and dermal nests of melanocytes. The overall architecture is benign.

Melanoma.

The epidermis shows a regular pattern of nests of melanocytes. There are scattered brown pigment granules within the nests. The dermis shows a prominent pigment network.
Melanoma.
